title: Multiple sclerosis
authors: Reich DS, Lucchinetti CF, Calabresi PA
primary_function: 7 years of forward citations; pathology/imaging depth including Lucchinetti lesion heterogeneity. Bridges 2018–2024 citation mass not yet in Jakimovski 2024 neighborhood.
rationale: Retained alongside R2 (Jakimovski 2024) because the 2018 paper has accumulated 7 years of forward citations that the 2024 paper has not yet generated. Papers published 2018–2024 that are important to the field will have cited Reich et al. as background. Also has distinctive emphasis on lesion heterogeneity and the Lucchinetti Mayo Clinic pathology tradition not replicated in the 2024 review.

title: Multiple sclerosis (Lancet Seminar 2024)
primary_function: Current field-wide overview. Covers pathophysiology, diagnosis, DMTs, progressive MS, prodrome, biomarkers. Replaces 2018 reviews as primary comprehensive anchor.
topics_covered: TOPIC-00, TOPIC-02, TOPIC-01, TOPIC-05, TOPIC-06, TOPIC-08, TOPIC-09
rationale: Primary field-wide expansion anchor; most recent comprehensive review; covers pathophysiology, diagnosis, DMTs, progressive MS, prodrome, and biomarkers. Reference list reflects the field's current consensus on what literature is canonical across all clinical and mechanistic topics.

title: MS progression: new mechanism-driven framework
authors: Kuhlmann T, Moccia M, Cohen JA, Mowry EM et al. (Intl Advisory Committee)
primary_function: Progressive MS continuum. Author list includes Mowry, Correale (Latin America), Marrie, Graves. Most geographically diverse high-profile MS progressive disease paper.
rationale: Included specifically for author list diversity in addition to intellectual content. International Advisory Committee authorship embeds perspectives and citations from Latin American (Correale), population outcomes (Marrie), and progressive disease (Mowry) research traditions. Reference list contributes geographic and perspectival diversity not achievable from US/European-anchored seeds alone.

title: Blood and CSF biomarkers for MS: emerging clinical applications
primary_function: Captures very recent biomarker literature (NfL, GFAP, CHI3L1, panels) not yet accumulated in seed neighborhoods. Tier 3 bridge for biomarker and monitoring topics.
rationale: Very recent (2025) biomarker review capturing NfL, GFAP, CHI3L1, and multi-analyte panel literature not yet accumulated in seed neighborhoods. Seeds MS-T1-013 and MS-T1-014 are 2018 and 2021; this anchor bridges the 2022–2025 biomarker acceleration. Critical for surfacing emerging serum biomarker monitoring papers before they accumulate sufficient cross-seed connectivity.

title: Health disparities and inequities in MS and related disorders
primary_function: Equity subdomain expansion. Equity papers are structurally disconnected from basic science citation network. Without a dedicated expansion source the equity literature is systematically missed by cross-seed filter.
rationale: Dedicated equity expansion source addressing a principled algorithmic limitation: equity papers are structurally disconnected from the basic science and clinical trial citation network. The cross-seed connectivity filter systematically misses equity literature because these papers are not cited by DMT trials or pathophysiology papers. A dedicated expansion source is the correct mechanism for capturing this structurally isolated but substantively important subfield.

title: Diagnosis of MS: 2024 revisions of McDonald criteria
authors: Montalban X, Oh J, Banwell B et al.
primary_function: Most geographically diverse author team of any MS paper in this set: Amezcua (Hispanic MS), Saylor (Africa/global), Banwell (pediatric), Fujihara (Japan), Correale (Latin America). Diagnostic and biomarker literature from a global perspective.
rationale: Most geographically diverse author team in the expansion source set: includes Amezcua (Hispanic MS), Saylor (sub-Saharan Africa), Banwell (pediatric), Fujihara (Japan/Asia), and Correale (Latin America). Reference list therefore reflects the field's diagnostic literature from a global perspective not achievable from the predominantly North American and European seed set. Also the authoritative source for 2024 McDonald criteria revisions.

title: Pathways to cures for MS: research roadmap 2022
primary_function: Stop/Restore/End framework; community-consensus curation of foundational references across TOPIC-08–TOPIC-17; patient input from 300+ people with MS; endorsed by 30+ MS societies.
rationale: Community-consensus strategic review; reference list encodes the field's own assessment of what is foundational across all cure-oriented research areas. Promoted from T1 seed to framing anchor to unlock reference-list expansion.

title: Refined Pathways to Cures Research Roadmap 2024
primary_function: 2024 update; smoldering MS and remyelination additions; most recent community-consensus view of research priorities; multi-regional authorship.
rationale: Updated community-consensus review; reference list adds recency signal especially for smoldering MS and progressive MS treatments post-2022. Complements R7 with 2-year increment of emerging frontier literature.
